FILE: packages/aqua-charts-wc/src/helpers/microChartOptions.ts
================================================
export function MicroChartOptions() {
  return {    
    getOptionsAxisMicro(axis: 'xAxis' | 'yAxis') {
      const titles = {
        xAxis: {
          labels: {
            enabled: false,
          },
          title: {
            text: null,
          },
          startOnTick: false,
          endOnTick: false,
          tickPositions: [],
        },
        yAxis: {
          endOnTick: false,
          startOnTick: false,
          labels: {
            enabled: false,
          },
          title: {
            text: null,
          },
          tickPositions: [0],
        },
      };

      return titles[axis];
    },
  }
};



================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-charts-wc/src/helpers/palettesConfig.ts
================================================
export function Palette(component: any) {
  return {
    getLocalPalette() {
      let localPalette: any;      
      if (component.palette === 'CustomColors') {
        localPalette = this.hasCustomPalette() ? component.palette : 'Cyan';
      } else {
        localPalette = component.palette;
      }
      return localPalette;
    },
    hasCustomPalette() {
      return component.customPalette.active && component.customPalette?.palette.length >= 6;
    },
  }
};



================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-charts-wc/src/helpers/titles.ts
================================================
import cloneDeep from 'lodash.clonedeep';
import { Options, XAxisOptions, YAxisOptions } from "highcharts";
import { TitlesUtils, LimitTitle, TitleTextAlignEnum, ComponentTile, SubTitleTextAlignEnum } from "../types/helpers/titles";

export default function Titles(component?: ComponentTile, limitTitle?: LimitTitle): TitlesUtils {
  return {
    hideTitles(
      componentTitles,
      options
    ) {
      const opts = cloneDeep(options);

      componentTitles.forEach((element) => {
        if (!opts?.[element]) return;

        if (element !== 'xAxis' && element !== 'yAxis') {
          opts[element].text = '';
          return;
        }
    
        const axis = opts[element];        
        if (!Array.isArray(axis)) {
          axis.title.text = '';
          return;
        }        
        axis.forEach((item: XAxisOptions | YAxisOptions) => {
          item.title.text = '';
        });          
      });
    
      return opts;
    },
    
    setTitleStyles(options) {
      const opts = cloneDeep(options);
      const styles = {
        title: {
          ...opts?.title,
          style: {
            color: 'var(--color-ink-base)',
            font: 'var(--font-size-xl) "Trebuchet MS", Verdana, sans-serif',
          },
        },
        subtitle: {
          ...opts?.subtitle,
          style: {
            color: 'var(--color-paper-darker)',
            font: 'var(--font-size-m) "Trebuchet MS", Verdana, sans-serif',
          },
        },
      };

      const optionsStyles = { ...opts, ...styles };
      return optionsStyles;
    },

    getTitleConf(): Options {
      const requestedText = component?.options?.title?.align;
      const validAlign = limitTitle?.alignTitle?.includes(requestedText as TitleTextAlignEnum) ? requestedText : limitTitle?.alignTitle[0] || TitleTextAlignEnum.CENTER;

      const requestedSubtitle = component?.options?.subtitle?.align;
      const validSubtitle = limitTitle?.alignSubtitle?.includes(requestedSubtitle as SubTitleTextAlignEnum) ? requestedSubtitle : limitTitle?.alignSubtitle[0] || SubTitleTextAlignEnum.CENTER;
      return {
        title: {
          ...component?.options?.title,
          align: validAlign,
        },
        subtitle: {
          ...component?.options?.subtitle,
          align: validSubtitle,
        }
      }
    }
  }
};



================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-charts-wc/src/helpers/tooltip.js
================================================
export default {
  props: {
    tooltipPositionerX: {
      type: Number,
      default: 0,
    },
    tooltipPositionerY: {
      type: Number,
      default: 12,
    },
    positionerX: {
      type: Number,
      default: 2,
    },
    positionerY: {
      type: Number,
      default: -16,
    },
    tooltipSize: {
      type: String,
      default: '16px',
    },
    tooltipWrap: {
      type: Boolean,
      default: true,
    },
    percentByTotal: {
      type: Boolean,
      default: false,
    },
    pointFormat: {
      type: String,
      default: '',
    },
    suffix: {
      type: String,
      default: '',
    },
  },
  methods: {
    getTooltipPositioner(labelWidth) {
      return {
        x: (this.$refs.chart.chart.chartWidth - labelWidth) / 2 + this.tooltipPositionerX,
        y: this.$refs.chart.chart.plotHeight / 2 + this.tooltipPositionerY,
      };
    },
    getTooltipPositionerMicro(labelWidth) {
      return {
        x: (this.$refs.chart.chart.chartWidth - labelWidth) / 2 + this.positionerX,
        y: this.$refs.chart.chart.plotHeight / 2 + this.positionerY,
      };
    },
    getTooltipSizeMicroCharts() {
      const actualSize = this.tooltipSize.match(/\d/g)?.join('');
      return actualSize > 8 ? '8px' : actualSize;
    },
    getTooltip(percentByTotal = false, isMicro = false, isActivityGauge = false) {
      let tooltipContent = '';
      const sourceName = isActivityGauge ? 'series' : 'point';

      switch (true) {
        case this.pointFormat !== '':
          tooltipContent = this.pointFormat;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && !percentByTotal && this.tooltipWrap && !isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `<div style="white-space:pre-wrap;"><span>{${sourceName}.name}</span> <b style="color:{point.color}; font-size:2em; font-weight: bold; text-align: center; width: 100%">{point.y}</b></div>`;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && !percentByTotal && this.tooltipWrap && isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `<div style="white-space:pre-wrap;"><span>{${sourceName}.name}</span> <br/> <b style="color:{point.color}; font-size:${this.getTooltipSizeMicroCharts()}; font-weight: bold; text-align: center; width: 100%">{point.y}</b></div>`;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && !percentByTotal && !this.tooltipWrap && !isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `<div><span>{${sourceName}.name}</span> <b style="color:{point.color}; font-size:2em; font-weight: bold; text-align: center; width: 100%">{point.y}</b></div>`;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && percentByTotal && !isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `{${sourceName}.name}<br><span style="font-size:2em; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold">{point.y}</span><br><span style="font-size:1em; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold">({point.percent}% of Total)</span>`;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && !percentByTotal && !isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `{${sourceName}.name}<br><span style="font-size:2em; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold">{point.y}</span><br>`;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && percentByTotal && isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `{${sourceName}.name}<br><span style="font-size:${this.getTooltipSizeMicroCharts()}; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold">{point.y}</span><br><span style="font-size:${this.getTooltipSizeMicroCharts()}; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold">({point.percent}% of Total)</span>`;
          break;

        case this.pointFormat === '' && !percentByTotal && isMicro:
          tooltipContent = `{${sourceName}.name}<br><span style="font-size:${this.getTooltipSizeMicroCharts()}; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold">{point.y}</span>`;
          break;

        default:
          tooltipContent = `{${sourceName}.name}<br><span style="font-size:2em; color: {point.color}; font-weight: bold; text-align: center; width: 100%">{point.y}</span>`;
          break;
      }

      return tooltipContent;
    },
    getTooltipCircularOptions(percentByTotal = false, isMicro = false, isActivityGauge = false) {
      return {
        borderWidth: 0,
        backgroundColor: 'none',
        shadow: false,
        style: {
          fontSize: !isMicro ? this.tooltipSize : this.getTooltipSizeMicroCharts(),
        },
        valueSuffix: this.suffix,
        positioner: !isMicro ? this.getTooltipPositioner : this.getTooltipPositionerMicro,
        pointFormat: this.getTooltip(percentByTotal, isMicro, isActivityGauge),
        headerFormat: '',
        useHTML: true,
      };
    },
    getTooltipMicroOptions() {
      return {
        useHTML: true,
        outside: true,
      };
    },
  },
};

import { Options } from "highcharts";
export { LegendOptions } from 'highcharts';

export enum LegendLayoutEnum {
    HORIZONTAL = 'horizontal',
    VERTICAL = 'vertical',
}

export enum LegendAlignEnum {
    RIGHT = 'right',
    CENTER = 'center',
    LEFT = 'left'
}

export enum LegendVerticalAlignEnum {
    BOTTOM = 'bottom',
    TOP = 'top',
    MIDDLE = 'middle',
}

export interface LimitLegend {
    [LegendLayoutEnum.HORIZONTAL]?: {
        align?: LegendAlignEnum[];
        verticalAlign?: LegendVerticalAlignEnum[];
    }
    [LegendLayoutEnum.VERTICAL]?: {
        align?: LegendAlignEnum[];
        verticalAlign?: LegendVerticalAlignEnum[];
    }
}

export interface ComponentLegend {
    options?: Options;
}

import { Options } from "highcharts";

export type ComponentTitleKey = 'xAxis' | 'yAxis' | 'title' | 'subtitle';

export interface TitlesUtils {
  hideTitles: (
    componentTitles: ComponentTitleKey[],
    options: Options
  ) => Options;

  setTitleStyles: (options: Options) => Options;

  getTitleConf: () => Options;
}

export enum TitleTextAlignEnum { 
  LEFT = 'left',
  CENTER = 'center',
  RIGHT = 'right',
}

export enum SubTitleTextAlignEnum { 
  LEFT = 'left',
  CENTER = 'center',
  RIGHT = 'right',
}

export interface LimitTitle {
  alignTitle?: TitleTextAlignEnum[];
  alignSubtitle?: SubTitleTextAlignEnum[];
}

export declare function Titles(limitTitle?: LimitTitle): TitlesUtils;

export interface ComponentTile {
  options?: Options;
}

FILE: packages/aqua-charts-wc/src/utils/commons/ColorPalettes.ts
================================================
import Highcharts, { ColorType } from 'highcharts';
import { Colors, Palettes, RevertedColors, Status } from '../../types/helpers/palette/colorPalette';
import { CustomPalette } from '../../types/global/global';
 

const defaultCustomPalette: CustomPalette = {
    active: false,
    palette: [],
    asignSameColor: false,
    colorIndex: 0,
};

/**
 * Assigns monochromatic and specific colors to passed data
 * @param data Data to set the colors
 * @param palette Palette name
 * @returns Data manipulated with colors
 */
export const assignColors = (
    data: Array<{ [key: string]: string | number[] | ColorType }>,
    palette: Palettes,
    customPalette = defaultCustomPalette,
    useCustomColors = false,
    isBubbles = false,
    isLine = false,
) =>
    data.map((value: { [key: string]: any }, index: number) => {
        const itemData = value;        
        if (typeof itemData !== 'object') {
            console.error(
                'Aq-charts: The type of data passed is incorrect please check the data: is expected of type object and is received of type: ...',
                typeof itemData,
            );
            return;
        }

        // TODO recieve empty color from palette config
        if (itemData.emptyColor) {
            itemData.color = '#B6B8BE';
            return itemData;
        }
        
        if (palette in Colors && !itemData?.customColor) {
            if (isBubbles) {
                itemData.marker = {
                    lineColor: 'transparent',
                };
            }

            // TODO Do this by default for all input base #colors
            if (palette === Palettes.STATUS) {
                const status = itemData.status as Status;
                if (status in Colors[Palettes.STATUS]) {
                    const color = Colors[Palettes.STATUS][status] || '#E5E5E5';
                    itemData.color = color;
                }
            }

            // ? Si el index es menor que la cantidad de colores
            // TODO Dejar que cada área pase su paletta default, o una opción de revertir orden
            else if (index < Colors[palette].length) {
                const colors = !isLine ? Colors[palette] : RevertedColors[palette];
                itemData.color = colors[index];
            }

            else {
                const group = Math.floor(index / Colors[palette].length);
                const colors = !isLine ? Colors[palette] : RevertedColors[palette];
                itemData.color = Highcharts.color(colors[index - group * Colors[palette].length])
                    .setOpacity(1 - group / 10)
                    .get();
            }
        } 
        // ! PARTE 2: Paletas personalizadas 
        else if (
            palette.toString() === 'CustomColors' &&
            customPalette?.active &&
            !itemData?.customColor
        ) {
            const localPalette = customPalette?.palette;
            if (customPalette?.asignSameColor) {
                itemData.color = localPalette[customPalette?.colorIndex];
            } else if (index < localPalette.length) {
                itemData.color = localPalette[index];
            } else {
                const group = Math.floor(index / localPalette.length);
                itemData.color = Highcharts.color(localPalette[index - group * localPalette.length])
                    .setOpacity(1 - group / 10)
                    .get();
            }
        } else if (itemData?.customColor && useCustomColors) {
            const color = itemData.customColor || '#E5E5E5';
            itemData.color = color;
        }
        return itemData;
    });

/**
 * Assigns monochromatic with one color and specific colors to passed data
 * @param data Data to set the colors
 * @param palette Palette name
 * @param colorIndex Color index in the list of colors
 * @returns
 */
export const asignSameColor = (
    data: Array<{ [key: string]: string | ColorType }>,
    palette: Palettes,
    colorIndex: number,
    customPalette = defaultCustomPalette,
    useCustomColors = false,
) =>
    data.map((value: { [key: string]: any }, index: number) => {
        const itemData = value;
        if (typeof itemData === 'object') {
            if (palette in Colors) {
                if (palette === Palettes.STATUS) {
                    const status = itemData.status as Status;
                    if (status in Colors[Palettes.STATUS]) {
                        const color = Colors[Palettes.STATUS][status] || '#E5E5E5';
                        itemData.color = color;
                    }
                } else if (palette === Palettes.SPECIFICCOLORS) {
                    if (index < Colors[palette].length) {
                        itemData.color = Colors[palette][index];
                    } else {
                        const group = Math.floor(index / Colors[palette].length);
                        itemData.color = Highcharts.color(
                            Colors[palette][index - group * Colors[palette].length],
                        )
                            .setOpacity(1 - group / 10)
                            .get();
                    }
                } else {
                    itemData.color = Colors[palette][colorIndex];
                }
            } else if (palette.toString() === 'CustomColors' && customPalette?.active) {
                const localPalette = customPalette?.palette;
                if (customPalette?.asignSameColor) {
                    itemData.color = localPalette[customPalette?.colorIndex];
                } else if (index < localPalette.length) {
                    itemData.color = localPalette[index];
                } else {
                    const group = Math.floor(index / localPalette.length);
                    itemData.color = Highcharts.color(localPalette[index - group * localPalette.length])
                        .setOpacity(1 - group / 10)
                        .get();
                }
            } else if (palette.toString() === 'CustomColors' && useCustomColors) {
                const color = itemData.customColor || '#E5E5E5';
                itemData.color = color;
            }
        } else {
            console.error(
                'Aq-charts: The type of data passed is incorrect please check the data: is expected of type object and is received of type:',
                typeof itemData,
            );
        }
        return itemData;
    });

export const asignOneColor = (palette: Palettes, customPalette = defaultCustomPalette) => {
    const localPalette =
        palette.toString() === 'CustomColors' && customPalette?.active
            ? customPalette?.palette
            : Colors[palette];
    const color = Object.entries(localPalette)[0][1];
    return color;
};

export const getPalette = (palette: Palettes, customPalette = defaultCustomPalette) => {
    const localPalette =
        palette.toString() === 'CustomColors' && customPalette?.active
            ? customPalette?.palette
            : Colors[palette];
    return localPalette;
};

FILE: packages/aqua-charts-wc/src/utils/commons/Theme.ts
================================================
export default (type: string) => {
  const theme = {
    colors: ['#F87C7E', '#E44E51', '#C7242B', '#94101B', '#690713', '#45040E'],
    chart: {
      backgroundColor: {
        // linearGradient: [0, 0, 500, 500],
        stops: [
          [0, 'rgb(255, 255, 255)'],
          [1, 'rgb(240, 240, 255)'],
        ],
      },
    },
    title: {
      style: {
        color: '#000',
        font: 'bold 16px "Trebuchet MS", Verdana, sans-serif',
      },
    },
    subtitle: {
      style: {
        color: '#666666',
        font: 'bold 12px "Trebuchet MS", Verdana, sans-serif',
      },
    },
    legend: {
      itemStyle: {
        font: '9pt Trebuchet MS, Verdana, sans-serif',
        color: 'black',
      },
      itemHoverStyle: {
        color: 'gray',
      },
    },
  };
  if (type === 'area') {
    theme.colors = ['#119AEB', '#5ABEFC', '#8AD4FF'];
  }
  if (type === 'alert') {
    theme.colors = ['#C7242B', '#F2C840', '#24A852'];
  }

  return theme;
};

# [AquaDs React](https://masivapp.notion.site/Quick-Start-1f31de3976b9809891d8ca8aeaa4d5fa)

![version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40aqua-ds%2Freact)

![AquaDs](https://img.notionusercontent.com/ext/https%3A%2F%2Fs3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic.notion-static.com%2Fbd2cb463-be69-4b79-a901-ef0ff01e283c%2Fsimbol_aqua.png/size/w=160?exp=1758922476&sig=gdBTpSOkMJyg1mAZzp5llZWD1HFegTHzrbLMcpbS3M0&id=1f21de39-76b9-80c9-9a15-007a540a7f73&table=custom_emoji)


✨ **Design together. Build together. Speak the same language.** ✨



`npm i @aqua-ds/react`

---


Integrate seamlessly into React applications using the dedicated package

## Installation

To use Aqua DS in a **React** project, you must have an existing React application running with support for the latest Node and npm versions.

Once your project is ready, you only need to **install the package**, **import the components**, and **use them directly in JSX.**

> ⚠️ When using Aqua Web Components directly in a React (Next.js) application, they can only be rendered on the client side. This means you must load them inside a Client Component to ensure proper initialization and avoid hydration errors.

**We are currently working on a solution to provide better SSR support**
> 

1. Install the React package:

```bash
npm i @aqua-ds/react
```

You’re ready to use Aqua in React!

# Using Aqua DS Components

Here's a basic example of how to use an Aqua DS component in a React application:

```jsx
// App.jsx
import { AqButton } from '@aqua-ds/react';

export const ButtonSet = () => {
	const handleClick = (e: MouseEvent) => {
		console.log('AqButton click from React', e);
	}

  return (
    <div>
      <AqButton variant="primary" type="submit" onClick={(e) => handleClick(e)}>
        <em className="aq-icon-settings"></em>Button
      </AqButton>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Here’s how you can use components from the official list:

[Components](https://masivapp.notion.site/Components-2011de3976b9801388eadaacd389ee67) 

## Naming Convention in React

In React, Aqua DS components must be imported and used in **PascalCase** to ensure full compatibility with JSX. Unlike Vue, where components are often written in kebab-case, React requires component names to follow PascalCase (a variant of CamelCase) when rendered in JSX.

```jsx
...
import { AqButton } from '@aqua-ds/react';
...

return (
  ...
  <AqButton variant="primary">Button</AqButton> <!-- ✅ Correct -->
	...
)
```

<aside>
❗

Avoid using `<aq-button>` in templates. It may not render correctly in some environments.

</aside>

## Handling Component Events

Aqua DS components support standard React event handling. You can use event listeners like `onClick`, `onChange`, etc., as you would with native React elements.

The `<AqButtonSplit>` component from Aqua DS emits custom events such as `onClickLeft`, `onClickRight`, and `onClickItem`. These events can be handled using React's `onEventName` pattern, following camelCase convention.

Here is a full example:

```jsx
import { AqButtonSplit } from '@aqua-ds/react';

export const ButtonSplitSet = ({ items }) => {
  const handleaqclickLeft = (e) => {
    console.log('Left button clicked', e);
  };

  const handleaqclickRight = (e) => {
    console.log('Right button clicked', e);
  };

  const handleaqclickItem = (e) => {
    console.log('Dropdown item clicked', e.detail); // Custom event data
  };

  return (
    <AqButtonSplit
      items={items || []}
      variant="success"
      size="medium"
      badge={{ number: 2, state: 'default' }}
      icon="aq-icon-send-money"
      onClickLeft={(e) => handleaqclickLeft(e)}
      onClickRight={(e) => handleaqclickRight(e)}
      onClickItem={(e) => handleaqclickItem(e)}
    >
      Button
    </AqButtonSplit>
  );
};

```

<aside>
ℹ️

**Note:** Ensure the component you're using emits the event you're listening for. Event names follow the standard camelCase format in React.

</aside>

<aside>
💡

Always refer to the documentation of each individual component for a complete list of supported events, their purpose, and usage examples.

</aside>

## Passing Properties to Components

When using Aqua DS components in React, you can pass properties directly as JSX props, just like with native React components.

Here's a practical example with the `<AqButtonSplit />` component:

```jsx
import { AqButtonSplit } from '@aqua-ds/react';

const items = [
  { id: '1', name: 'Option 1' },
  { id: '2', name: 'Option 2' },
];

const badge = { number: 2, state: 'default' };

export const ButtonSplitExample = () => {
  const handleaqclickLeft = (e: MouseEvent) => {
    console.log('Left button clicked', e);
  };

  const handleaqclickRight = (e: MouseEvent) => {
    console.log('Right button clicked', e);
  };

  const handleaqclickItem = (e: CustomEvent) => {
    console.log('Item clicked:', e.detail);
  };

  return (
    <AqButtonSplit
      items={items}
      variant="success"
      size="medium"
      badge={badge}
      icon="aq-icon-send-money"
      onClickLeft={handleaqclickLeft}
      onClickRight={handleaqclickRight}
      onClickItem={handleaqclickItem}
    >
      Button
    </AqButtonSplit>
  );
};
```

<aside>
✅

Properties like `items`, `variant`, `badge`, and `icon` are passed as standard props. Event handlers are bound using the `onEventName` convention.

</aside>

## Two Way Binding

In React, two-way binding is typically achieved by using `useState` to store the component's value and updating it via an event handler like `onChange`.

Here is an example using `<AqCheckbox>`:

```jsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { AqCheckbox } from '@aqua-ds/react';

export const CheckboxExample = () => {
  const [checkedValue, setCheckedValue] = useState<string | undefined>(undefined);

  const handleValueChanged = (event: CustomEvent) => {
    const value = event.detail;
    setCheckedValue(value);
    console.log('Checkbox changed to:', value);
  };

  return (
    <AqCheckbox
      id-checkbox="checkbox-1"
      name="checkbox-1"
      label="This is a checkbox 1"
      icon="aq-icon-message"
      isRequired
      info="This is an information tooltip"
      value-checkbox="option-1"
      onChange={handleValueChanged}
    />
  );
};
```

✅ Use `useState` to track checkbox value changes via the `onChange` event handler.

🧠 `value-checkbox` represents the selected value passed to the component.

**On this page**

✨ **Design together. Build together. Speak the same language.** ✨

# [AquaDS Vue3](https://masivapp.notion.site/Quick-Start-1f31de3976b9809891d8ca8aeaa4d5fa)

![version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40aqua-ds%2Fvue)
![lastUpdated](https://img.shields.io/npm/last-update/%40aqua-ds%2Fvue)


![AquaDs](https://img.notionusercontent.com/ext/https%3A%2F%2Fs3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic.notion-static.com%2Fbd2cb463-be69-4b79-a901-ef0ff01e283c%2Fsimbol_aqua.png/size/w=160?exp=1758922476&sig=gdBTpSOkMJyg1mAZzp5llZWD1HFegTHzrbLMcpbS3M0&id=1f21de39-76b9-80c9-9a15-007a540a7f73&table=custom_emoji)


✨ **Design together. Build together. Speak the same language.** ✨



`npm i @aqua-ds/vue`

---

Works smoothly with Vue 3 using on-demand imports and Vite configuration.

# Installation

To install Aqua DS in a Vue 3 project using NPM:

1. Ensure you have a Vue 3 project initialized and are using the latest versions of Node.js and NPM.
2. Install Aqua DS Package for Vue:
    
    ```bash
    npm i @aqua-ds/vue
    ```
    
3. Configure **`vite.config.js`:**
4. If using Vite, you must add the following so Vue knows which tags should be treated as **Web Components** instead of **Vue components** during the template compilation process (Learn more at [Vue and Web Components | Vue.js](https://vuejs.org/guide/extras/web-components.html#using-custom-elements-in-vue)):
    
    ```jsx
    ...
    import vueJsx from '@vitejs/plugin-vue-jsx'
    ...
    
    export default defineConfig({
    	...
      plugins: [
    		vue({
          template: {
            compilerOptions: {
              isCustomElement: (tag) => tag.startsWith('aq-'),
            },
          },
        }),
        vueJsx(),
        ...
        ]
      }
    );
    ```
    

# Using Aqua DS Components in Vue 3

Once you’ve installed Aqua DS and set up your project, you can begin using components in your Vue 3 templates.

Here’s how you can use components from the official list:

[Components](https://www.notion.so/Components-2011de3976b9801388eadaacd389ee67?pvs=21) 

**Example:** `<aq-button>`

```html
<!-- anyFile.vue -->
<script lang="ts">
import { defineComponent } from 'vue';
import { AqButton } from '@aqua-ds/vue';

export default defineComponent({
	components: {
    AqButton,
  },
  setup() {
	  const handleclick = (e: Event) => {
	    console.log('AqButton click from vue ', e);
	  };
	}
});
</script>

<template>
  <div class="container">
    <aq-button @click="handleclick" variant="primary" type="submit">
      <em class="aq-icon-settings"></em> Button
    </aq-button>
	</div>
</template>
```

<aside>
💡

**Tip:** Aqua DS supports tree-shaking, so importing only the components you use keeps your final bundle small.

</aside>

## Importing Components in Vue

To ensure full compatibility when using Aqua components in Vue.js, always import them explicitly inside a `defineComponent` block.

```html
<script lang="ts">
import { AqBadge } from '@aqua-ds/vue';
import { defineComponent } from 'vue';

export default defineComponent({
  components: {
    AqBadge,
  },
});
</script>

<template>
	...
  <aq-badge state="warning">Warning</aq-badge>
	...
</template>
```

## Handling Component Events

Aqua DS components emit **custom DOM events** that are fully compatible with Vue 3’s native event handling syntax. This means you can use `v-on:` or the shorthand `@` to listen for events, just like with any standard Vue component.

**Example: Listening to an Event**

```html
<script lang="ts">
import { AqButton } from '@aqua-ds/vue';
import { defineComponent } from 'vue';

export default defineComponent({
  components: { AqButton },
  setup() {
    const handleClick= (ev: Event) => {
      console.log('Handling button click event: ', ev);
    };
    return { 
		  handleClick
    };
  }
});
</script>
<template>
	<div>
	  <aq-button variant="primary" @click="handleClick">
	    Click Me
	  </aq-button>
  </div>
</template>
```

```html
<script lang="ts">
import { AqCheckbox } from '@aqua-ds/vue';
import { defineComponent } from 'vue';

export default defineComponent({
  components: { AqCheckbox },
  setup() {
    const handleValueChanged = (ev: Event) => {
      console.log('Handling checkbox custom aqChange from vue: ', ev);
    };
    return { 
		  handleValueChanged
    };
  }
});
</script>

<template>
	<div>
		<aq-checkbox 
			id-checkbox="checkbox-2" 
			name="checkbox-2" 
			label="This is a checkbox 2" 
			icon="aq-icon-message"
      is-required 
      info="This is an information tooltip" 
      value-checkbox="option-2" 
      @input="handleValueChanged"
    >
    </aq-checkbox>
	</div>
</template>
```

## Two-Way Binding

Aqua DS is compatible with Vue 3’s `v-model` for **two-way binding**, allowing your application to stay in sync with component values.

 **Example Using** `v-model`

```html
<script lang="ts">
import { AqCheckbox } from '@aqua-ds/vue';
import { defineComponent } from 'vue';

export default defineComponent({
  components: { AqCheckbox },
  setup() {
	  const selectedValue = ref();
	  return {
		  selectedValue,
	  }
  }
});
</script>
<template>
	<div>
		<aq-checkbox 
			id-checkbox="checkbox-2" 
			name="checkbox-2" 
			label="This is a checkbox 2" 
			icon="aq-icon-message"
      is-required 
      info="This is an information tooltip" 
      value-checkbox="option-2" 
      @input="handleValueChanged"
      v-model="selectedValue"
    >
    </aq-checkbox>
	</div>
</template>
```

<aside>
💡

Always refer to the documentation of each individual component for a complete list of supported events, their purpose, and usage examples.

</aside>

<aside>
ℹ️

Only **some** Aqua DS components are compatible with `v-model`, particularly those that deal with user input and require reactive state handling (such as form fields).

</aside>

## Passing Properties to Components

When using Aqua DS components in Vue 3, you can pass properties just like you would with any other component. Aqua DS supports standard HTML attributes, as well as custom component-specific props. Vue automatically converts kebab-case attributes into camelCase props internally.

```html
<script lang="ts">
import { AqButtonSplit } from '@aqua-ds/vue';
import { defineComponent, ref } from 'vue';
	
export default defineComponent({
  components: { AqButtonSplit },
  setup() {
    const handleaqclickLeft = (e: Event) => {
      console.log('Handling button-group custom aqclick from vue ', e);
    };
    const handleaqclickRight = (e: Event) => {
      console.log('Handling button-group custom aqclick from vue ', e);
    };
    const handleaqclickItem = (e: Event) => {
      console.log('Handling button-group custom aqclick from vue ', e);
    };
    const items = ref([{
	    id: "id1",
	    name: "option1"
	  },
	  {
	    id: "id2",
	    name: "option2"
	  }]);

    return {
      handleaqclickLeft,
      handleaqclickRight,
      handleaqclickItem,
      items
    };
  },
});
</script>

<template>
	<div class="section">
	  <aq-button-split
		  :items="items"
		  variant="success"
		  size="medium"
		  icon="aq-icon-send-money"
		  @clickLeft="handleAqClickLeft"
		  @clickRight="handleAqClickRight"
		  @clickItem="handleAqClickItem"
	  >
		  Button
	  </aq-button-split>
  </div>
</template>
```

This is a practical example of using the `<aq-button-split>` component in Vue 3. This includes:

- **Static props** like `variant`, `size`, and `icon`.
- **Dynamic props** passed via `v-bind` (shorthand `:`).
- **Event bindings** using Vue's `@` directive (`v-on` shorthand).

<aside>
⚠️

All Aqua DS component events are fully compatible with Vue 3’s native event binding syntax. You can listen to any custom event emitted by a component using either:

- `v-on:eventName`
- `@eventName` (shorthand for `v-on`)
</aside>

<aside>
ℹ️

Most Aqua DS component props are fully compatible with Vue’s `v-bind` directive, allowing you to dynamically bind data to the component’s attributes.

</aside>

## Implementing custom Slots

When we develop a component using the **Stencil API**, the `slot` element is implemented based on Web Component standards. This differs from **bindings** and **custom events**, which are adapted to work with framework-specific integrations.

When the component is transpiled into a **Vue component**, it is not a fully native Vue component but rather a wrapper around a Web Component. As a result, some Vue-specific features are not fully supported.

For example, when a developer uses the `v-slot` or `#slot` syntax on a **Vue component**, Vue expects to find a Vue-native slot. However, since the slot is defined using the Web Component specification (as per-Stencil), Vue is not aware of it.

To properly use our slots, developers must use the native Web Component slot syntax provided by the **Stencil API**—that is, using the `slot` attribute directly in the markup.

```html
 <aq-dropdown id="dropdown-1" :items="items" :popover-width="300">
  <aq-button slot="activator" is-outline
    ><i class="aq-icon-three-dots-vertical"></i
  ></aq-button>
</aq-dropdown>
```

When you do this, you'll gain access to the Web Component slot and be able to use it as needed.

If you're using **Vetur**, a linter, or any tool with strict Vue file validation, and it shows a syntax error, follow these steps:

### `“slot” attributes are deprecated`

Don’t worry about that error — it’s not caused by your code, nor is it related to our library. It’s just that Vue mistakenly thinks we’re using the Vue 2 API, but in reality, we’re targeting the Stencil API. To prevent and fix this warning, you just need to configure it properly in your `eslint.config` file:

```jsx
// eslint.config.ts
rules: {
  // ...
  'vue/no-deprecated-slot-attribute': 'off',
  // ...
}
```

## Vue Component Naming Convention

In Aqua DS, **all Vue components must be used in lowercase** (e.g., `aq-button`) when declared in your templates. This is required to ensure maximum compatibility across platforms, especially when using components in HTML-like syntaxes where uppercase tags are not recognized properly.

Even though you import the component in PascalCase in your JavaScript (`AqButton`), it should **always be used in lowercase** in your Vue templates:

```html
<template>
  <aq-button variant="primary">Button</aq-button> <!-- ✅ Correct -->
</template>
```

<aside>
❗

Avoid using `<AqButton>` in templates. It may not render correctly in some environments.

</aside>

# Important Notes

### **Component Names:**

Aqua DS components must be used in **kebab-case** (`aq-button`, not `AqButton`) to ensure full compatibility in Vue templates.

### **Tree Shaking:**

To reduce your final bundle size, only import the components you use.

Example (in your main file or component script):

```html
<script>
...
import {
  AqDropdown,
  AqButton,
  AqSubheading,
  AqCheckbox,
  AqBadge,
} from '@aqua-ds/vue'
...
</script>
```

### 


✨ **Design together. Build together. Speak the same language.** ✨

import '@aqua-ds/web-components/styles/aqua-web-components.css';

export * from '@aqua-ds/web-components/enums';

FILE: packages/aqua-vue/lib/plugin.ts
================================================
// plugins/directives.ts

import { Plugin } from 'vue';
import { setupDirectives } from '@aqua-ds/web-components/directives';
import { Directives } from '@aqua-ds/web-components/enums';

interface LibraryDirectivesOptions {
  directives: Directives[]; // ['aq-dialog', 'aq-spinner', ...]
}

export const LibraryDirectives: Plugin = {
  install(_, options?: LibraryDirectivesOptions) {
    if (!options || !options.directives || options.directives.length === 0) {
      console.warn(
        '[LibraryDirectives] ⚠️ Directives not found. Please declare app.use(LibraryDirectives, { directives: [...] })',
      );
      return;
    }

    setupDirectives(options.directives);
  },
};

FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/readme.md

# AquaDS WebComponents

![version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40aqua-ds%2Fweb-components)

 ✨ **Design together. Build together. Speak the same language.** ✨

 </center>


`npm i @aqua-ds/web-components`

---

Use the components directly in HTML without any framework by installing the Aqua Web Components package.

# Installation

Aqua DS Web Components can be installed in two different ways depending on your project’s needs:

## **Using NPM**

For projects using Node.js with a build system (like Vite, Webpack, etc.):

1. Make sure your project is initialized and supports modern JavaScript.
2. Install Aqua DS Web Components:

```bash
npm i @aqua-ds/web-components
```

1. Import and define the components in your JavaScript entry point:
    
    <aside>
    ℹ️
    
    When use **defineCustomElements** you will subscribe all the web-components on the website. That will enable the possibility of access to any web-component at any moment. **That not affects the app performance.**
    
    </aside>
    

```jsx
import { defineCustomElements } from '@aqua-ds/web-components/loader';
defineCustomElements();
```

## Using CDN

For simpler setups (e.g., pure HTML without a build step):

1. Include the Aqua DS script in your HTML:

```jsx
<script type="module" src=[url-to-cdn]></script>
```

<aside>
⚠️

If you need access to the URL AQUA CDN to connect to Aqua’s private cdn, please send an email to the FrontOps team *frontops@masiv.com*

</aside>

<aside>
💡

Note: CDN is ideal for prototyping or static environments.

</aside>

# Using Components

Aqua DS offers a wide range of Web Components that can be used across compatible environments. Each component comes with defined props, slots, and events to provide flexible UI behavior.

Here’s how you can use components from the official list:

[Components](https://masivapp.notion.site/Components-2011de3976b9801388eadaacd389ee67) 

Example: `<aq-button>`

```html
<aq-button variant="primary" type="submit">
  <em class="aq-icon-settings"></em>Submit
</aq-button>
```

## Handling Events

Aqua DS components emit custom events to allow interaction and dynamic behavior in your applications. When using Web Components directly (e.g., in HTML or JavaScript), you can listen to these events using standard DOM event listeners.

```html
<aq-button variant="primary" type="submit" id="aqButton">
  <em class="aq-icon-settings"></em>Submit
</aq-button>

<script>
	const aqButton = document.getElementById("aqButton");
	
	aqButton.addEventListener('click', (event)=> {
		console.log("Click from AqButton: ", event.detail);
	});
</script>
```

Each Aqua DS component defines its own set of custom events to support interactive and reactive behavior. These events are designed to reflect meaningful user interactions or internal state changes.

<aside>
ℹ️

Always refer to the documentation of each individual component for a complete list of supported events, their purpose, and usage examples.

</aside>

Using the correct event names and handlers ensures proper integration within your app logic and facilitates a seamless user experience.

## Passing Properties to Web Components

In Aqua DS, you can pass properties (also known as "props" or "attributes") directly to Web Components via HTML attributes or JavaScript.

These properties control the component’s behavior, appearance, and logic. Each component has its own set of supported props, which you can find in its documentation.

### **HTML Example**

```html
<aq-text-field
	id="aqTextField"  
	type="number"
	placeholder="This has slot with left content" 
	icon="aq-icon-settings" 
	label="This is a label" 
	sublabel="Sub"
  helper-text="This is a helper text"
  info="This is a tooltip"
  char-counter
	is-required
	is-textarea>
</aq-text-field>
```

### Javascript Example

```jsx
const aqTextField = document.getElementById("aqTextField");
aqTextField.icon = "aq-icon-email";
aqTextField.label = "TextField Label"
```

- Boolean props (like `is-disabled` or `is-required`) can be added without a value in HTML.

### Complex data types as property

In WebComponents some props only works when are manually set via Javascript, depending on the component’s internal logic.

```html
<aq-button-split
    id="aqButtonSplit"
    type="button"
    variant="default"
    size="medium"
    info="This is a tooltip" >
    <em class="aq-icon-send-money"></em> Button
</aq-button-split>
<script>
  const buttonSplit = document.querySelector('#aqButtonSplit');
  
  // Setting a JS-only attribute (complex object)
  buttonSplit.items = [
    { id: "1", name: "Option 1" },
    { id: "2", name: "Option 2" },
    { id: "3", name: "Option 3" }
  ];
</script>
```

<aside>
ℹ️

Always check the official documentation of each component to understand which props are available and how they behave

</aside>

✨ **Design together. Build together. Speak the same language.** ✨

import { Config } from '@stencil/core';
import { vueOutputTarget } from '@stencil/vue-output-target';

import dotenvPlugin from 'rollup-plugin-dotenv';

export const config: Config = {
  namespace: 'aqua-web-components',
  minifyJs: true,
  sourceMap: false,
  buildEs5: 'prod',
  extras: {
    experimentalSlotFixes: true,
    // To lazy load components
    // enableImportInjection: true,
  },
  globalStyle: 'src/globals/main.scss',
  globalScript: 'src/globals/scripts/init.ts',
  outputTargets: [
    {
      type: 'www',
      serviceWorker: null, // disable service workers
    },
    // {
    //   type: 'docs-readme',
    // },
    {
      type: 'dist',
      esmLoaderPath: '../loader',
      dir: 'dist',
      empty: true,
    },
    // {
    //   type: 'dist-custom-elements',
    //   externalRuntime: true,
    //   dir: './components-build',
    //   generateTypeDeclarations: true,
    // },

      externalRuntime: false,
      dir: './components-build',
      generateTypeDeclarations: true,
    },
    vueOutputTarget({
      componentCorePackage: '@aqua-ds/web-components',
      proxiesFile: '../aqua-vue/lib/components.ts',
      includeImportCustomElements: true,
      includePolyfills: false,
      includeDefineCustomElements: false,
      componentModels: [
        {
          elements: ['aq-autocomplete'],
          targetAttr: 'value',
          event: 'changevalue',
        },
      ],
    }),
    reactOutputTarget({
      stencilPackageName: '@aqua-ds/web-components',
      customElementsDir: 'components',
      outDir: '../aqua-react/lib/components',
    }),
    angularOutputTarget({
      componentCorePackage: '@aqua-ds/web-components',
      directivesProxyFile: '../aqua-angular/src/lib/awc/components.ts',
      directivesArrayFile: '../aqua-angular/src/lib/awc/index.ts',
      outputType: 'standalone',
      // valueAccessorConfigs: angularValueAccessorBindings,
    }),
  ],
  plugins: [
    dotenvPlugin(),
    sass({
      includePaths: ['node_modules'],
    }),
  ],
  testing: {
    browserHeadless: 'shell',
  },
};

FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/plugins/fixAngularImport.js
================================================
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

module.exports = function () {
  const filePath = path.resolve('../aqua-angular/src/lib/awc/components.ts');

  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
    console.error(`Find file: ${filePath}`);
  }

  try {
    let fileContent = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');

    // Verificar si ya está corregido
    if (fileContent.includes("@aqua-ds/web-components/loader")) {
      console.log(`Angular file Ok 💋: ${filePath}`);
    }

    // Reemplazar el contenido del archivo
    fileContent = fileContent.replace(
      /import type { Components } from ['"][^'"]+['"];/g,
      `import type { Components } from '@aqua-ds/web-components/loader';`
    );

    // Escribir el contenido actualizado
    fs.writeFileSync(filePath, fileContent, 'utf8');

    console.log(`File updated: ${filePath}`);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Error: ${error}`);
  }
};

angularFix();

 * to consume components of this package as outlined in the `README.md`.
 */

FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-button/aq-button.scss
================================================
@use '@aqua-ds/style-guidelines/scss/variables/color' as colors;
@use '@aqua-ds/style-guidelines/scss/variables/font' as fonts;
@use '@aqua-ds/style-guidelines/scss/variables/spacing' as spacing;
@use '@aqua-ds/style-guidelines/scss/variables/box-shadow' as box-shadow;

$aq-button-variables: (
  'border-color': colors.$color-paper-light,
  'text-color': colors.$color-ink-base,
  'background-color': transparent,
  'hover-background-color': colors.$color-paper-lighter,
  'disabled-background-color': colors.$color-white,
  'disabled-text-color': colors.$color-paper-dark,
  'spinner-color': colors.$color-paper-light,
);

:root {
  @each $name, $value in $aq-button-variables {
    --aq-button-#{$name}: #{$value};
  }
}

$aq-button-border-color: var(--aq-button-border-color);
$aq-button-text-color: var(--aq-button-text-color);
$aq-button-background-color: var(--aq-button-background-color);
$aq-button-hover-background-color: var(--aq-button-hover-background-color);
$aq-button-disabled-background-color: var(--aq-button-disabled-background-color);
$aq-button-disabled-text-color: var(--aq-button-disabled-text-color);
$aq-button-spinner-color: var(--aq-button-spinner-color);

@mixin button-variant($name, $text-color, $color-base, $color-dark, $color-light, $color-lighter) {
  &.#{$name} {
    --aq-button-text-color: #{$color-base};
    --aq-button-background-color: #{$color-base};

    --aq-button-border-color: #{$color-base};
    --aq-button-hover-background-color: #{$color-dark};

    --aq-button-disabled-background-color: #{$color-light};
    --aq-button-disabled-text-color: #{$text-color};

    --aq-button-spinner-color: #{colors.$color-white};

    &:hover.is-outline,
    &:hover.is-plain,
    &:focus.is-outline,
    &:focus.is-plain {
      --aq-button-text-color: #{$color-base};
      --aq-button-hover-background-color: #{$color-lighter};
    }

    &:focus:not(.is-plain, .is-outline, :active) {
      background-color: $color-base;
    }

    &.is-disabled.is-outline,
    &.is-disabled.is-plain,
    &.is-loading.is-outline,
    &.is-loading.is-plain {
      --aq-button-text-color: #{$color-base} !important;

      --aq-button-disabled-background-color: #{colors.$color-white};
      --aq-button-disabled-text-color: #{$color-light};

      --aq-button-border-color: #{$color-light};
      --aq-button-spinner-color: #{$color-light};
    }

    &:not(.is-plain, .is-outline) {
      --aq-button-text-color: #{colors.$color-white};
    }
  }
}

aq-button {
  height: fit-content;
}

.aq-button {
  $self: &;

  font-family: fonts.$font-family-basic;
  font-size: fonts.$font-size-s;
  line-height: fonts.$font-line-height-7;

  border: spacing.$spacing-size-basic solid transparent;
  border-radius: spacing.$spacing-size-minor;
  font-weight: fonts.$font-weight-semi-bold;

  color: $aq-button-text-color;
  background-color: $aq-button-background-color;
  height: spacing.$spacing-size-big;

  cursor: pointer;
  padding: calc(spacing.$spacing-size-small * 3) spacing.$spacing-size-medium;
  gap: spacing.$spacing-size-minor;

  display: inline-flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  position: relative;

  transition: 0.3s;

  @include button-variant('primary', colors.$color-white, colors.$color-primary-base, colors.$color-primary-dark, colors.$color-primary-light, colors.$color-primary-lighter);

  @include button-variant('success', colors.$color-white, colors.$color-success-base, colors.$color-success-dark, colors.$color-success-light, colors.$color-success-lighter);

  @include button-variant('danger', colors.$color-white, colors.$color-danger-base, colors.$color-danger-dark, colors.$color-danger-light, colors.$color-danger-lighter);

  @include button-variant('warning', colors.$color-white, colors.$color-warning-base, colors.$color-warning-dark, colors.$color-warning-light, colors.$color-warning-lighter);

  &.small {
    font-size: fonts.$font-size-xs;
    padding: spacing.$spacing-size-minor spacing.$spacing-size-short;
    height: spacing.$spacing-size-large;
  }

  &.large {
    font-size: fonts.$font-size-m;
    padding: calc(spacing.$spacing-size-short + spacing.$spacing-size-basic) spacing.$spacing-size-medium;
    height: spacing.$spacing-size-greater;
  }

  &.is-outline {
    border-color: $aq-button-border-color;
    color: $aq-button-text-color;
    background-color: transparent;
  }

  &.is-plain {
    color: $aq-button-text-color;
    border-color: transparent;
    background-color: transparent;
  }

  &:active:not(.is-disabled, .is-loading) {
    background: $aq-button-hover-background-color;
    box-shadow: box-shadow.$shadow-active;
  }

  &:hover {
    background: $aq-button-hover-background-color;
    box-shadow: box-shadow.$shadow-basic;
  }

  &:focus-visible {
    outline: none;
  }

  &:focus {
    background: $aq-button-hover-background-color;
    box-shadow: box-shadow.$shadow-focus;
    outline: unset;
  }

  &:focus,
  &:active {
    box-shadow: box-shadow.$shadow-focus;
    z-index: 1;
  }

  &.is-loading {
    position: relative;
    color: transparent !important;
    &::after {
      position: absolute;
      box-sizing: inherit;
      animation: rotateSpinner 0.5s linear infinite;

      border-radius: 9999px;
      content: '';
      display: block;
      height: 1em;
      width: 1em;
      rotate: 45deg;

      left: calc(50% - 0.5em);
      top: calc(50% - 0.5em);

      border: calc(spacing.$spacing-size-basic * 2) solid $aq-button-spinner-color;
      border-right-color: transparent;
      border-top-color: transparent;
    }
  }

  &.is-disabled,
  &.is-loading {
    box-shadow: none;
    color: $aq-button-disabled-text-color;
    background: $aq-button-disabled-background-color;
    cursor: default;
    pointer-events: none;
  }

  &__disclosure {
    padding-left: spacing.$spacing-size-small;
  }

  &:has(.aq-button__disclosure) {
    padding: calc(spacing.$spacing-size-small * 3) !important;
  }
}



================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-button/aq-button.tsx
================================================
import { Component, Prop, h, Element } from '@stencil/core';
import { StyleClassMap } from '../../../types/StyleClassMap';
import { emitEvent } from 'utils/helpers/eventEmitter';
import { IButton } from './interfaces/IButton';
import { Alignment, Size, DefaultColors } from 'enum';
import { ButtonType } from './enum/ButtonType';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-button',
  styleUrl: 'aq-button.scss',
})
export class AqButton implements IButton {
  @Prop() type: IButton['type'] = ButtonType.BUTTON;
  @Prop() variant: IButton['variant'] = DefaultColors.DEFAULT;
  @Prop() size: IButton['size'] = Size.MEDIUM;

  @Prop() isDisabled = false;

  @Prop() hasDisclosure = false;
  @Prop() isLoading = false;
  @Prop() isOutline = false;
  @Prop() isPlain = false;

  @Prop() info = '';
  @Prop() customClass = '';

  @Element() hostElement!: HTMLElement;

  private getTooltip() {
    return (
      this.info && (
        <aq-tooltip config={{ placement: Alignment.TOP }}>
          <span>{this.info}</span>
        </aq-tooltip>
      )
    );
  }

  private handleClick = (event: Event) => {
    event.stopPropagation();
    if(this.isDisabled) return;
    emitEvent('click', this.hostElement, { event }, event);
  };

  private getStyleClassMap(): StyleClassMap {
    return {
      'aq-button': true,
      'is-disabled': this.isDisabled,
      'is-loading': this.isLoading,
      'is-outline': this.isOutline,
      'is-plain': this.isPlain,

      [this.size]: this.size !== Size.MEDIUM,
      [`${this.variant}`]: this.variant !== DefaultColors.DEFAULT,
      [this.customClass]: true,
    };
  }

  render() {
    const cssClass = this.getStyleClassMap();
    const tooltip = this.getTooltip();

    return (
      <button onClick={e => this.handleClick(e)} type={this.type} disabled={this.isDisabled} class={cssClass}>
        <slot></slot>
        {this.info && tooltip}
        {this.hasDisclosure && <span class="aq-button__disclosure aq-icon-chevron-down"></span>}
      </button>
    );
  }
}

import { SizeType } from 'components';
import { ButtonType } from '../enum/ButtonType';
import { CoreColorsType, DefaultColorsType } from 'types';

export interface IButton {
  type: ButtonType;

  variant: CoreColorsType | DefaultColorsType;
  size: SizeType;

  isDisabled: boolean;
  hasDisclosure: boolean;
  isLoading: boolean;
  isOutline: boolean;
  isPlain: boolean;

  info: string;
  customClass: string;
}

  render() {

================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-button-split/aq-button-split.tsx
================================================
import { Component, Prop, h, Element, Event, EventEmitter } from '@stencil/core';
import { StyleClassMap } from 'types/StyleClassMap';
import { DefaultColors, PopoverWidths, Size } from 'enum';
import { IButtonSplit } from './interfaces/IButtonSplit';
import { ButtonType } from './enum/ButtonType';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-button-split',
  styleUrl: 'aq-button-split.scss',
})
export class AqButtonSplit implements IButtonSplit {
  @Prop() type: IButtonSplit['type'] = ButtonType.BUTTON;
  @Prop() variant: IButtonSplit['variant'] = DefaultColors.DEFAULT;
  @Prop() size: IButtonSplit['size'] = Size.MEDIUM;

  @Prop() isLoading = false;
  @Prop() isDisabled = false;
  @Prop() isOutline = false;
  @Prop() isPlain = false;
  @Prop() info = '';
  @Prop() items: { id: string; name: string }[] = [];

  @Prop() customClass = '';

  @Event() clickLeft: EventEmitter<{ event: globalThis.Event }>;
  @Event() clickRight: EventEmitter<{ event: globalThis.Event }>;
  @Event() clickItem: EventEmitter<{ value: string }>;

  @Element() hostElement!: HTMLElement;

  private onClickLeft(event: globalThis.Event) {
    this.clickLeft.emit({ event });
  }

  private onClickRight(event: any) {
    if (event.detail.value === undefined) {
      this.clickRight.emit({ event });
    }
  }

  private onClickItem(e, itemId: string) {
    e.stopPropagation();
    this.clickItem.emit({ value: itemId });
  }

  private cssClassAqButtonLeft(): StyleClassMap {
    return {
      'aq-button-split__left-button': true,
    };
  }

  private cssClassAqButtonDivider(): StyleClassMap {
    return {
      'aq-button-split__divider': true,
      'aq-button-split__divider__outline': this.isOutline || this.isPlain,
      [`aq-button-split__divider--${this.variant}`]: this.isOutline || this.isPlain || this.variant === DefaultColors.DEFAULT,
      [`aq-button-split__divider--${this.size}`]: true,
    };
  }

  private getTooltip() {
    return (
      this.info && (
        <aq-tooltip config={{ placement: 'top' }}>
          <span>{this.info}</span>
        </aq-tooltip>
      )
    );
  }

  render() {
    const cssClassAqButtonLeft = this.cssClassAqButtonLeft();
    const cssClassAqButtonDivider = this.cssClassAqButtonDivider();
    const tooltip = this.getTooltip();
    return (
      <div class={`aq-button-split ${this.isDisabled ? 'is-disabled' : ''}`}>
        <div>
          {this.info && tooltip}
          <div class="aq-button-split__container">
            <aq-button
              onClick={e => this.onClickLeft(e)}
              class={cssClassAqButtonLeft}
              variant={this.variant}
              size={this.size}
              isOutline={this.isOutline}
              isPlain={this.isPlain}
              type={this.type}
              isDisabled={this.isDisabled}
              isLoading={this.isLoading}
              customClass={this.customClass}
            >
              <div style={{ visibility: this.isLoading ? 'hidden' : 'visible', zIndex: '10' }}>
                <slot></slot>
              </div>
            </aq-button>
            {!this.isLoading && <aq-divider class={cssClassAqButtonDivider} orientation="vertical" />}
            <div class="aq-button-split__right">
              <aq-dropdown id="dropdown-3" placement="bottom" popoverWidth={PopoverWidths.AUTO} is-disabled={this.isDisabled || this.isLoading} onClick={e => this.onClickRight(e)}>
                <aq-button
                  slot="activator"
                  variant={this.variant}
                  size={this.size}
                  isOutline={this.isOutline}
                  isPlain={this.isPlain}
                  type={this.type}
                  customClass={this.customClass}
                >
                  <em class="aq-icon-chevron-down" style={{ visibility: this.isLoading ? 'hidden' : 'visible' }}></em>
                </aq-button>
                <div slot="content">
                  {this.items.map((item: any) => (
                    <aq-dropdown-item class="dropdown_item" id={item.id} onClick={e => this.onClickItem(e, item.id)}>
                      {item.name}
                    </aq-dropdown-item>
                  ))}
                </div>
              </aq-dropdown>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }
}

================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-dropdown/aq-dropdown-host/aq-dropdown.tsx
================================================
import { Component, h, Prop, State, Event, EventEmitter, Element, Watch } from '@stencil/core';
import { IDropdown } from './interfaces/IDropdown';
import { Placement } from 'tippy.js';
import { PopoverWidths } from 'enum/PopoverWidths';
import type { IDropdownItem } from 'interfaces/common/IDropdownItem';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-dropdown',
  styleUrl: 'aq-dropdown.scss',
})
export class AqDropdown implements IDropdown {
  @Element() el: HTMLElement;

  // Props
  @Prop({ mutable: true, reflect: true }) items: IDropdownItem[] = [];
  @Prop() label = '';
  @Prop() idParent = '';
  @Prop() dataCallback = null;
  @Prop() closeOnClickOutside = true;
  @Prop() isDisabled = false;
  @Prop() placement: Placement = 'right';
  @Prop() popoverWidth: PopoverWidths | number = PopoverWidths.DEFAULT;

  @Watch('items')
  onItemsChange(newVal: any[]) {
    this.localItems = newVal;
  }

  // State
  @State() isVisible = false;
  @State() activatorElement = null;
  @State() showListMenu = false;
  @State() popoverConfig = {
    hideOnClick: false,
    hideOnClickOutside: this.closeOnClickOutside,
  };

  localItems: any[] = [];

  // Events
  @Event() itemSelection: EventEmitter<
    {
    label: string;
    icon?: string;
    class?: string;
    classIcon?: string;
    callback?: (item: {
      label: string;
      icon?: string;
      class?: string;
      classIcon?: string;
    }) => void;
  }>;

  // Methods
  addClassActive() {
    const parent = this.getParentElement;
    if (parent) {
      parent.classList.add('aq-dropdown--active');
    }
  }
  removeClassActive() {
    const parent = this.getParentElement;
    if (parent) {
      parent.classList.remove('aq-dropdown--active');
    }
  }
  validateClick(item: IDropdownItem) {
    if (item.callback && typeof item.callback === 'function') {
      const localItem = {...item};
      delete localItem.callback;  
      item.callback(localItem);
    } else {
      this.itemSelection.emit(item);
    }
  }
  onVisible(value: boolean) {
    this.isVisible = value;
  }
  getItemClassLength(item: any) {
    return item?.class?.length > 0;
  }
  getItemIconLength(item: any) {
    return item?.icon?.length > 0;
  }
  getItemClassIconLength(item: any) {
    return item?.classIcon?.length > 0;
  }
  setVisible(evt: CustomEvent<boolean>) {
    this.onVisible(evt.detail);
  }
  renderActivator() {
    return this.getLabelLength ? <aq-button>{typeof this.label === 'string' ? this.label : ''}</aq-button> : <slot name="activator" />;
  }
  setDropdownStyle(item: any) {
    return {
      [item.class]: item.class && this.getItemClassLength(item),
    };
  }
  setIconStyle(item: any) {
    return {
      [item.icon]: true,
      [item.classIcon]: item.classIcon && this.getItemClassIconLength(item),
    };
  }
  handleShow() {
    setTimeout(() => {
      const listMenu = this.el.querySelector('aq-list-menu');
      const event = new CustomEvent('update');
      listMenu.dispatchEvent(event);
    }, 50);
  }

  get getLabelLength() {
    return typeof this.label === 'string' && this.label.length > 0;
  }
  get getItemsLength() {
    return this.localItems.length > 0;
  }
  get getParentElement() {
    return document.getElementById(this.idParent as string) || this.el;
  }
  get getDropdownStyle() {
    return {
      'aq-dropdown': true,
      'aq-dropdown--active': this.isVisible,
    };
  }
  get getActivator() {
    return this.getLabelLength ? this.renderActivator() : <slot name="activator" />;
  }
  get getPopoverConfig() {
    return {
      ...this.popoverConfig,
      placement: this.placement,
    };
  }
  get getContent() {
    const items = this.localItems.map((item: IDropdownItem, index: number) => (
      <aq-dropdown-item class={this.setDropdownStyle(item)} key={index} onSelection={() => this.validateClick(item)}>
        {item.icon && this.getItemIconLength(item) ? <em class={this.setIconStyle(item)}></em> : null}
        <span class="aq-dropdown-item__text">{item.label}</span>
      </aq-dropdown-item>
    ));
    return items;
  }

  componentDidLoad() {
    this.localItems = [...this.items];
  }

  componentDidRender() {
    requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      const activator = this.el.querySelector('span');
      if (activator) {
        this.activatorElement = activator;
        this.activatorElement.children[0].isDisabled = this.isDisabled;
      }
    });
  }

  // Render
  render() {
    const getDropdownStyle = this.getDropdownStyle;
    const getItemsLength = this.getItemsLength;
    const isDynamic = !getItemsLength;
    const activator = this.getActivator;
    const getPopoverConfig = this.getPopoverConfig;
    const getContent = this.getContent;

    return (
      <div class={getDropdownStyle}>
        {/* Label Button */}
        <span>{activator}</span>
        {/* Dropdown Content */}
        <aq-popover slot="popover" disabled={this.isDisabled} onAqshow={() => this.handleShow()} trigger={[this.activatorElement]} config={getPopoverConfig}>
          <aq-list-menu is-dynamic={isDynamic} popoverWidth={this.popoverWidth} container={this.el} activator={this.activatorElement}>
            <slot name="header" slot="header" />
            {getItemsLength ? <div slot="content">{getContent}</div> : <slot name="content" slot="content" />}
            <slot name="footer" slot="footer" />
          </aq-list-menu>
        </aq-popover>
      </div>
    );
  }
}



================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-dropdown/aq-dropdown-host/interfaces/IDropdown.ts
================================================
import { PopoverWidths } from 'enum/PopoverWidths';
import { IPopover } from 'interfaces/common/IPopoverConfig';
import { Placement } from 'tippy.js';
import { StyleClassMap } from 'types/StyleClassMap';

export interface IDropdown {
  el: HTMLElement;

  // Props
  items: Array<any>;
  label: string;
  idParent: string;
  dataCallback: Function;
  closeOnClickOutside: boolean;
  isDisabled: boolean;
  placement: Placement;
  popoverWidth?: PopoverWidths | number;

  // State
  isVisible: boolean;
  popoverConfig: IPopover;
  activatorElement: HTMLElement;

  addClassActive(): void;
  removeClassActive(): void;
  validateClick(item: any): void;
  onVisible(value: boolean): void;
  getItemClassLength(item: any): boolean;
  getItemIconLength(item: any): boolean;
  getItemClassIconLength(item: any): boolean;
  setDropdownStyle(item: any): StyleClassMap;
  setIconStyle(item: any): StyleClassMap;

  get getLabelLength(): boolean;
  get getItemsLength(): boolean;
  get getParentElement(): HTMLElement;
  get getDropdownStyle(): StyleClassMap;
  get getActivator(): HTMLElement;
  get getPopoverConfig(): IPopover;
}

import { Component, Element, Event, EventEmitter, h } from '@stencil/core';

@Component({

})
export class AqDropdownItem {

    this.selection.emit();
  }

  render() {
    return (
      <div class="aq-dropdown-item" onClick={() => this.clicked()}>
        <slot> </slot>

    );

FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-menu/aq-list-menu/aq-list-menu-host.tsx
================================================
import { Component, Element, Host, Listen, Prop, State, h } from '@stencil/core';
import { IVirtualScrollConfig } from 'interfaces/IVirtualScrollConfig';
import { PopoverWidths } from 'enum/PopoverWidths';
import { IConfigDropdown } from 'interfaces/common/IConfigDropdown';
import { IlistMenu } from './interfaces/IListMenu';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-list-menu',
  styleUrl: 'aq-list-menu-host.scss',
})
export class AqListMenu implements IlistMenu {
  @Element() el: HTMLElement;

  @Prop() elementRow: boolean;
  @Prop() additionalConfig: IVirtualScrollConfig;
  @Prop() popoverWidth: PopoverWidths | number;
  @Prop() container: HTMLElement;
  @Prop() activator: HTMLElement;
  @Prop() config: IConfigDropdown;
  @Prop() isDynamic: boolean;
  @Prop() allowSelectAll: boolean;

  @State() virtualScroll;
  @State() slot: ChildNode;

  get getAllowSelectAll() {
    return <div>{this.allowSelectAll && <aq-button>Select All 11</aq-button>}</div>;
  }

  @Listen('update')
  onUpdate() {
    this.executeEventToVirtualList('update');
  }
  @Listen('updateContent')
  onUpdateContent(evt: any) {
    this.executeEventToVirtualList('updateContent', { detail: evt.detail });
  }

  private executeEventToVirtualList(evtName: string, payload?: any) {
    const virtualList = this.el.querySelector('aq-virtual-list');
    const event = new CustomEvent(evtName, payload);
    virtualList.dispatchEvent(event);
  }

  render() {
    const getAllowSelectAll = this.getAllowSelectAll;
    return (
      <Host>
        <div class="aq-list-menu-host">
          <aq-virtual-list is-dynamic={this.isDynamic} popoverWidth={this.popoverWidth} container={this.container} activator={this.activator}>
            <slot name="header" slot="header"></slot>
            <div>{getAllowSelectAll}</div>
            <slot name="content" slot="content"></slot>
            <slot name="footer" slot="footer"></slot>
          </aq-virtual-list>
        </div>
      </Host>
    );
  }
}

import { IConfigDropdown } from 'interfaces/common/IConfigDropdown';

export interface IlistMenu {
  elementRow: boolean;
  additionalConfig: IVirtualScrollConfig;
  popoverWidth: PopoverWidths | number;
  container: HTMLElement;
  activator: HTMLElement;
  config: IConfigDropdown;
  isDynamic: boolean;

}

================================================
FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-toggle/aq-toggle.tsx
================================================
import { Component, Host, h, State, Prop, Watch, Element } from '@stencil/core';
import { IToggle } from './interfaces/IToggle';
import { UniqueIdGenerator } from 'utils/helpers/uidGenerator';
import { emitEvent } from 'utils/helpers/eventEmitter';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-toggle',
  styleUrl: 'aq-toggle.scss',
})
export class AqToggle implements IToggle {
  @Prop({ mutable: true, reflect: true }) value = false;
  @Prop() isDisabled = false;
  @Prop() hasLabel = false;
  @Prop() helperText: string = '';
  @Prop() idToggle: string = '';
  
  @State() uid = '';

  @Element() hostElement!: HTMLElement;

  @Watch('idToggle')
  watchHandler() {
    this.vadidateId();
  }
  getStyleToggle() {
    return {
      'aq-toggle': true,
      'aq-toggle--disabled': this.isDisabled,
    };
  }
  vadidateId() {
    this.uid = this.idToggle.length > 0 ? this.idToggle : new UniqueIdGenerator().generateId();
  }
  componentWillLoad() {
    this.vadidateId();
  }

  updateToggle(event: Event) {
    event.preventDefault();
    event.stopPropagation();
    if (!this.isDisabled) {
      this.value = !this.value;
      emitEvent('input', this.hostElement, { value: this.value }, event);
      emitEvent('change', this.hostElement, { value: this.value }, event);
    }
  }

  get getIsHelperTextVisible() {
    return !!this.helperText;
  }

  render() {
    const cssClassToggle = this.getStyleToggle();
    return (
      <Host class={cssClassToggle}>
        <div class="aq-toggle__content ">
          <label class="aq-toggle__element">
            <input type="checkbox" checked={this.value} value="true" id={this.uid} disabled={this.isDisabled} onClick={event => this.updateToggle(event)}></input>
            <span class="aq-toggle__slider"></span>
          </label>
          {this.hasLabel && (
            <label class="aq-toggle__label" htmlFor={this.uid}>
              <div hidden={!this.value}>
                <slot name="on">On</slot>
              </div>
              <div hidden={this.value}>
                <slot name="off">Off</slot>
              </div>
            </label>
          )}
        </div>
        {this.getIsHelperTextVisible && (
          <div class="aq-toggle__helper-text">
            <aq-helper-text><span slot="text">{this.helperText}</span></aq-helper-text>
          </div>
        )}
      </Host>
    );
  }
}

export interface IToggle {
  value: boolean;
  isDisabled: boolean;
  uid: string;
  hasLabel: boolean;
  getStyleToggle(): StyleClassMap;
  updateToggle(event: Event): void;
  vadidateId(): void;
}

FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-tree-menu/aq-tree-menu-core/aq-tree-menu.tsx
================================================
import { Component, Event, Method, Prop, State, Watch, h } from '@stencil/core';
import { ITreeMenu } from './interfaces/ITreeMenu';
import { UniqueIdGenerator } from 'utils/helpers/uidGenerator';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-tree-menu',
  styleUrl: 'aq-tree-menu.scss',
})

export class AqTreeMenu implements ITreeMenu {
  @Prop() readonly options: ITreeMenu['options'] = [];

  @State() localOptions: ITreeMenu['options'] = [];
  @Event() toggleItem: ITreeMenu['toggleItem'];

  @Watch('options')
  onOptionsUpdate() {
    this.refreshLocalOptions();
  }

  // Component API
  @Method()
  async setOptions (options: ITreeMenu['options']) {
    this.localOptions = [ ...options]
  }
  @Method()
  async getOptions() {
    return this.localOptions;
  }
  @Method()
  async collapseAll() {
    this.localOptions = this.localOptions.map(item => {
      if(!item?.isDisabled) return { ...item, isOpen: false };
      }
    );
  }
  
  private handleToggleSubItem(e: CustomEvent) {
    const clickedItem = e.detail as any;
    this.toggleItem.emit({ clickedItem, localOptions: this.localOptions })
  }
  private refreshLocalOptions() {
    this.localOptions = this.options.map((child) => ({ ...child, isOpen: child.isOpen || false }));
  }
  private generateUID() {
    return new UniqueIdGenerator().generateId();
  }

  componentWillLoad() {
    // Create a local copy of the options prop
    this.refreshLocalOptions();
  }
  
  render() {
    return (
      <div class="aq-tree-menu">
        {
          this.localOptions.map((item: any, idx: number) => {
            return <aq-tree-menu-item
              key={idx}
              item={item}
              idItem={item.idItem || `${this.generateUID()}`}
              level={0}
              onToggleSubItem={(e) => this.handleToggleSubItem(e)}
            >
            </aq-tree-menu-item>
          })
        }
      </div>
    );
  }
}

import { EventEmitter } from "@stencil/core";

export type TreeMenuOptionType = {
  idItem?: string | number;
  label?: string;
  
  isOpen?: boolean;
  isDisabled?: boolean;
  icon?: string;

  childrenItems?: TreeMenuOptionType[];
  level?: number;
}

export interface ITreeMenu {
  options: TreeMenuOptionType[];

  toggleItem: EventEmitter<{ clickedItem: TreeMenuOptionType, localOptions: TreeMenuOptionType[] }>
}

FILE: packages/aqua-web-components/src/components/actions/aq-tree-menu/aq-tree-menu-item/aq-tree-menu-item.tsx
================================================
import { Component, Prop, h, Element, Event, EventEmitter } from '@stencil/core';
import { UniqueIdGenerator } from 'utils/helpers/uidGenerator';

@Component({
  tag: 'aq-tree-menu-item',
  styleUrl: 'aq-tree-menu-item.scss',
})
export class AqTreeMenuItem {
  @Prop() readonly level: number = 0;
  @Prop({ mutable: true }) item: any;
  @Prop() idItem: number | string;

  @Event() toggleSubItem: EventEmitter<any>;

  @Element() hostElement!: HTMLElement;

  private handleClickOption() {
    // ! Updating current item by reference
    this.item.isOpen = !this.item.isOpen;
    this.item = {...this.item};
    
    this.toggleSubItem.emit(this.item)
  }
  private generateUID() {
    return new UniqueIdGenerator().generateId();
  }
  private getArrowIconTemplate() {
    const getArrowIcon = this.item.isOpen ? 'aq-icon-chevron-up' : 'aq-icon-chevron-down';
    return this.item?.childrenItems && this.item.childrenItems.length > 0 ? <em class={getArrowIcon}></em> : <span style={{ width: "0.875rem"}}></span>
  }
  private getOptionIcon() {
    return {
      [`${this.item.icon}`]: !!this.item.icon,
      ['aq-icon-folder']: !this.item.icon && !!this.item?.childrenItems,
    }
  }

  render() {
    const getItemClass = {
      'aq-tree-menu-item': true,
    }

    const getLevelButtonClass = {
      'aq-tree-menu-item__level': true,
    }

    const getButtonClass = {
      'aq-tree-menu-item__button': true,
      'aq-tree-menu-item__button--open': this.item.isOpen && !!this.item?.childrenItems,
      'aq-tree-menu-item__button--disabled': this.item.isDisabled,
    }

    return (
      <div class={getItemClass}>
        <button
          class={getButtonClass}
          onClick={() => this.handleClickOption()}
          disabled={this.item.isDisabled}
        >
          <div class={getLevelButtonClass} style={{ paddingLeft: `calc(${this.level}*var(--font-size-m))` }}>
            { this.getArrowIconTemplate() }
            <div class="aq-tree-menu-item__label">
              <em class={this.getOptionIcon()}></em>
              <span>
                {this.item.label}
              </span>
            </div>
          </div>
        </button>
        {
          this.item?.childrenItems && this.item?.childrenItems.length > 0 &&
          <div class={{
            'aq-tree-menu-item__children': true,
            'aq-tree-menu-item__children--visible': this.item.isOpen
          }}>
            {
              this.item?.childrenItems.map((subitem: any, idx: number) => {
                return <aq-tree-menu-item
                  key={idx}
                  item={subitem}
                  idItem={subitem.idItem || `${this.generateUID()}`}
                  level={this.level + 1}
                >
                </aq-tree-menu-item>
              })
            }
          </div>
        }
      </div>
    );
  }
}
